For that, okay, let's move on though to the next referral tool, which is a really good one, which is Newsletters and as a bonus with this package, folks are getting Sean Buck, my platinum member, excuse me, platinum now Titanium. So he gives you the exact blueprint on that. So but just briefly, why are referrals such an important tool? I mean, why are why are newsletters such an important tool for referrals? First, they're part of the foundation under the machine. So they impose discipline upon you in order to show up consistently and frequently. And so they are foundational. All right, they are a great place to showcase clients. And testimonials. And to recognize people who refer. So it gives you a media in which to dispense recognition thereby reinforcing to everybody. This is what you're supposed to do around here. The third thing it does is it provides an internal lead generation media for you to generate leads for specific product services promotions and offers. So Dan, one other thing about the newsletters is I notice that there's a lot of entertainment factor in in all of these newsletters here. What most people do if they go do this on their own. Or a lot of companies that sell the service in different niches because there's usually at least two or three vendors in real estate in insurance and so forth who are providing standardized We do it. We print it. We mail it. What you will find most of them doing is core deliverable centric news newsletters. People do it because they're fascinated with plumbing and therefore they think everybody else is fascinated with plumbing. Right. You know, I like my plumber when I need him, but you know, I'm really not all that into you know, the pipe of the month kind of conversation. But people do it all the time. Right. So if you think about what does a public read less than less. Right. What does a public read and what do they read most? Entertainment. They read entertainment, pop culture, gossip. Right. So they read for pleasure. Far more than they read for information. Right. The fiction category is much bigger than the non-fiction category. Go to Barnes and Noble Store and walk around. Right. Right. And count the shelf space. And that will tell you what the bulk of your newsletter ought to be. So it ought to be about people, places, things. It ought to be entertainment, crossword puzzles, game of the month. Recipes. Still the number one selling category of all books is cookbooks. A few are for people cook, but. They buy the books. They buy the books. Right. They may be hopeful gifts. I'm not sure, but you know, it's a big category. So really good newsletters are sort of readers digest. People magazine. Pop culture reference. So helpful hints, but not necessarily narrowed to how to keep your muffler from falling off. Right. And that has to be limited. It's only part because you need readership to win. Somebody has to want it. Somebody has to. Oh, I'll see what's. I can't wait to see what Dave has in here. That's a good question. I'll see what's. I can't wait to see what Dave right has in here this month. And. And the mistake is making it all technical and tied to the core deliverable.
